title: Today's AI Brief: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation

body.text: Today I am keeping this one simple: a quick, easy pass through the ai headlines, with each story getting its own moment. No grand lecture, no giant theory board with red string everywhere. Just what happened, why it is interesting, and a short read at the end.

First up: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation. Here is the headline. The main detail from the story is this: Save this story Save this story When Pope Leo XIV presented his first encyclical on artificial intelligence at the Vatican on Monday, he invited Christopher Olah, cofounder of Anthropic, to speak. The move signaled an unprecedented alliance between the Catholic church and Silicon Valley. But to understand how this partnership came about, we need to go back to Anthropic's founding. Why Anthropic? Anthropic launched i... In plain English, this is the kind of story that tells us what people in this corner of ai are paying attention to right now. The useful thing here is the shape of the AI story underneath it: who gets trust, who gets distribution, who pays the cost, and who has to explain the technology to everyone else over coffee.

Next: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI. Here is the headline. The main detail from the story is this: Sponsored In partnership with Ema Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution. Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next three years, 76% say their current operations and infrastructure can’t support that change. They cite a lack of readiness across people, processes, and workflows. The sticky tape problem T...
